<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>Review complete. Verified empirically: created a detached worktree of the branch in the scratchpad (main working tree untouched, now removed), ran the backend dashboard service tests (30 passed), the API + MCP dashboard tests (18 passed), the touched frontend tests (25 passed), full frontend `tsc --noEmit` (clean), and Ruff/mypy on the changed backend files (clean) — all against the local Postgres via `TEST_DATABASE_URL`.

Findings:

```json
[
  {
    "file": "frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx",
    "line": 81,
    "summary": "PLAUSIBLE — `flex=\"auto\"` on the Cols is inert and the xxl layout leaves a permanent right-side gap. Confirmed from antd v6 source (node_modules/antd/es/grid/col.js + grid/style/index.js): the span classes emit `flex: 0 0 x%; max-width: x%`; the `flex` prop only sets inline `flex: 1 1 auto`, which overrides the class flex but NOT the class `max-width`. So growth is always capped at the span width and `flex=\"auto\"` never lets a tile fill leftover row space.",
    "failure_scenario": "On a >=1600px (xxl) viewport, the five xxl={4} tiles occupy 5 x 16.67% = 83.3% of the row and cannot grow past max-width 16.67% — a ~17% empty strip sits to the right of Active Connections, contradicting the added comment 'Five equal-width tiles that wrap gracefully'. Fix: either drop flex=\"auto\" (dead prop) and accept the gap, use xxl spans that sum to 24 (e.g. 5/5/5/5/4 is impossible evenly — use flex-only layout without spans), or replace the Row/Col grid with a plain Flex/CSS grid for the 5-up row."
  },
  {
    "file": "frontend/src/components/dashboard/MetricCard.tsx",
    "line": 121,
    "summary": "PLAUSIBLE (edge) — the delta-badge guard `value !== null` doesn't suppress the badge when the value renders as the em-dash STRING. On the Avg. Duration card, Dashboard.tsx passes `value={formatDurationMs(avg_duration_ms)}`; a negative avg (clock skew: finished_at < started_at is not filtered in `_avg_duration_ms`, only NULLs are) formats to '—', which is a non-null string, so a delta badge can render under an em-dash value.",
    "failure_scenario": "One run in the window with finished_at earlier than started_at (worker clock skew / manual backfill) drives avg_duration_ms negative in both windows: card shows value '—' with a colored '+X% vs prior period' badge beneath it — a delta attached to visibly-no-data. Low severity; a `Run.finished_at >= Run.started_at` filter in _avg_duration_ms (backend/app/services/dashboard_service.py:259) or a numeric-value guard would close it."
  }
]
```

Everything else on the hunt list checked out clean:

- **(1) SQL**: `EXTRACT(epoch FROM finished_at - started_at)` + `avg()` is valid on Postgres (interval → numeric); confirmed by the passing tests against the real Postgres, including `test_avg_duration_over_finished_runs_only` (avg 2000.0 exact). **(8)** `avg` returns `Decimal`; `float(avg_s)` at dashboard_service.py:282 handles it — confirmed.
- **(2) Window boundaries**: current = `created_at >= since` (open), prev = `[since−w, since)`. A row at exactly `since` lands in current only — no double-count, no gap. (Pre-existing, unchanged: `_status_counts` windows on `Result.created_at` while `_total_runs`/`_avg_duration_ms` window on `Run.created_at`, so a run straddling the boundary can attribute its results and its run-count to different windows — consistent within each metric, not introduced by this PR.)
- **(3) Delta semantics**: previous=0/None → None (documented, tested); current=0 with previous>0 → −100% is sensible and renders (0 is not null). Score/rate deltas computed from already-rounded 1dp values — negligible.
- **(4) DeltaBadge zero**: renders gray "0% vs prior period", no arrow, no '+'. Acceptable. There is a stray leading space when delta===0 (`{null}{' '}0%...`) but it collapses in inline rendering — not a finding. `value !== null` correctly shows the runs delta at total_runs=0.
- **(6) MetricCard tests**: no dedicated MetricCard test file exists (grep: only Dashboard.tsx/Admin.tsx consume it); the "does not render unbacked prototype KPIs" Dashboard test still passes.
- **(7) API shape consumers**: MCP `get_health_score` (backend/app/mcp/server.py:210) builds its own dict from the dataclass (which has defaults) — unaffected; `frontend/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts` asserts labels/regex against the live app, its `/^\d+$/` Total-Runs locator still matches the value node; `backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py` doesn't touch the dashboard; `backend/tests/api/test_dashboard.py` asserts individual keys, not exact shape — all 18 pass. Frontend tsc confirms no other `Kpis` consumer.

Note: Admin.tsx also uses MetricCard (no delta props) — unaffected, confirmed by tsc + its optional props.</result>

content.text: PR 560's review is back — two plausible findings worth taking: the `flex="auto"` prop on antd Cols is inert (span `max-width` still caps growth, leaving a ~17% gap at xxl), and clock-skewed runs (`finished_at < started_at`) can produce a negative average that renders as an em dash with a delta badge under it. Applying both:

input.command: git -C /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ checkout feature/issue-352-dashboard-kpi-enrichment 2>&1 | tail -1

lastPrompt: Hey, morning.  Lets get started with week 8

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
p = '/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/services/dashboard_service.py'
s = open(p).read()
old = """    duration_s = func.extract("epoch", Run.finished_at - Run.started_at)
    stmt = (
        select(func.avg(duration_s))
        .select_from(Run)
        .where(
            Run.suite_id.in_(accessible),
            Run.created_at >= since,
            Run.started_at.is_not(None),
            Run.finished_at.is_not(None),
        )
    )"""
new = """    duration_s = func.extract("epoch", Run.finished_at - Run.started_at)
    stmt = (
        select(func.avg(duration_s))
        .select_from(Run)
        .where(
            Run.suite_id.in_(accessible),
            Run.created_at >= since,
            Run.started_at.is_not(None),
            Run.finished_at.is_not(None),
            # Clock skew / backfill can leave finished < started; a negative
            # interval would poison the mean (and the card renders it as junk).
            Run.finished_at >= Run.started_at,
        )
    )"""
assert old in s
s = s.replace(old, new, 1)
open(p, 'w').write(s)

p = '/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx'
s = open(p).read()
# Drop the inert flex="auto" + the xxl={4} gap; xl gives a clean 3+2 layout.
s = s.replace("""      {/* Five equal-width tiles that wrap gracefully on narrow screens. */}
      <Row gutter={[16, 16]}>""", """      {/* Five tiles: stacked on phones, 2-up on small screens, 3+2 at xl+. */}
      <Row gutter={[16, 16]}>""")
s = s.replace('<Col xs={24} sm={12} xl={8} xxl={4} flex="auto">', '<Col xs={24} sm={12} xl={8}>')
s = s.replace('<Col xs={24} sm={12} xl={12} xxl={4} flex="auto">', '<Col xs={24} sm={12} xl={12}>')
assert 'flex="auto"' not in s and 'xxl={4}' not in s
open(p, 'w').write(s)
print('ok')
EOF

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
p = '/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/tests/services/test_dashboard_service.py'
s = open(p).read()
old = """def test_avg_duration_none_when_nothing_finished(db_session: Any) -> None:"""
new = """def test_avg_duration_excludes_clock_skewed_runs(db_session: Any) -> None:
    \"\"\"finished < started (clock skew / backfill) must not poison the mean —
    and an all-skewed window is an honest None, not a negative duration.\"\"\"
    alice = _user(db_session)
    suite = _suite(db_session, alice)
    _run_with_results(
        db_session, suite, run_status="succeeded", result_statuses=["pass"], duration_s=-5.0
    )
    _run_with_results(
        db_session, suite, run_status="succeeded", result_statuses=["pass"], duration_s=2.0
    )

    summary = svc.dashboard_summary(db_session, user_id=alice.id, window_days=7)
    assert summary.kpis.avg_duration_ms == 2000.0  # the skewed run is excluded

    bob = _user(db_session)
    only_skew = _suite(db_session, bob)
    _run_with_results(
        db_session, only_skew, run_status="succeeded", result_statuses=["pass"], duration_s=-1.0
    )
    assert (
        svc.dashboard_summary(db_session, user_id=bob.id, window_days=7).kpis.avg_duration_ms
        is None
    )


def test_avg_duration_none_when_nothing_finished(db_session: Any) -> None:"""
assert old in s
s = s.replace(old, new, 1)
open(p, 'w').write(s)
print('ok')
EOF

input.command:
gh pr comment 560 --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
**/code-review pass (agent ran the branch in an isolated worktree; 30+18+25 tests + tsc + ruff/mypy all verified) — two PLAUSIBLE findings, both applied in the fixup commit:**
- ✅ Clock-skew guard: `_avg_duration_ms` now filters `finished_at >= started_at` — a skewed/backfilled run can no longer drive the mean negative (which rendered as an em dash **with** a delta badge beneath it). New regression test covers both the mixed and all-skewed windows.
- ✅ The `flex="auto"` on the KPI Cols was inert (antd span classes keep `max-width`, so tiles can never grow past their span) and `xxl={4}`×5 left a permanent ~17% right-side gap. Dropped both — the row is now stacked / 2-up / **3+2 at xl+** with no dead space at any width.

Reviewer verified clean: the Postgres `EXTRACT(epoch …)`/`avg` SQL, `Decimal→float`, window boundary semantics (a run at exactly `since` counts once, in the current window), delta semantics incl. `current=0 → −100%`, and **no consumer breakage** — MCP `get_health_score` builds from the dataclass (defaults absorb the new fields), the Playwright dashboard spec still matches, `e2e_smoke.py` untouched.

Also noted (pre-existing, not this PR): `_status_counts` windows on `Result.created_at` while run counts window on `Run.created_at`, so a boundary-straddling run can attribute results and run-count to different windows — consistent within each metric; leaving as-is.
EOF
)"

content.text:
All five Week-8 PRs are now complete on my side — reviewed by /code-review agents, findings applied, everything pushed. Final state:

**Ready to merge (in this order — B and C flip the gates):**
1. **[#556](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/556)** — CORS activation tests (*Fixes #385*). CI green.
2. **[#557](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/557)** — backend gap tests + **`--cov-fail-under=80`**. Backend lands at 98.3% / 1233 tests. Last push swapped the fake `hunter2` SMTP password that GitGuardian false-positived.
3. **[#558](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/558)** — honest frontend coverage (all of `src/` counted, 22 new tests) + **`lines: 80` gate, CI now runs `pnpm test:coverage`**. 87.8% lines at flip.
4. **[#559](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/559)** — catalog↔GX contract test (*Fixes #205*), fixture + drift-guard, proven red on planted defects.
5. **[#560](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/560)** — dashboard Avg. Duration card + real period-over-period deltas (*Fixes #352*), verified live against the local stack. The last fixup added a clock-skew guard on the duration mean and fixed an inert antd `flex` prop that left a dead gap at xxl widths.

The reviews earned their keep today — four confirmed defects caught before merge: a non-hermetic env test, a mutation-vacuous nav assertion, a fixture parse that would have aborted the whole pytest session, and the layout/skew pair on #560.

**Once #557 and #558 are merged, the Week-8 exit gate (≥80% enforced in CI across backend, API, frontend) is met.** I'll then do the close-out docs PR — flip the progress.md CI-gate row and the 🟡 audit rows, clear the issue register (#385/#205/#352), and propose closing #128 and the #177 epic. Just say the word after merging.

new = ("**Current week:** **Week 8 of 8 — Unit testing — COMPLETE (29/29, closed 2026-07-03) — ALL 8 WEEKS DONE; DataQ v1 is COMPLETE** "
       "(exit gate MET: ≥80% coverage gates enforced in CI — backend `--cov-fail-under=80` in `pyproject.toml`, 98.3%/1233 tests at flip after closing the four sub-80% modules (#557); "
       "frontend `lines: 80` over ALL of `src/` with CI running `pnpm test:coverage`, 87.8% (#558). The W8 batch #556–#560 also landed the CORS activation tests (closes #385), "
       "the catalog↔GX contract test pinning the check-editor catalog against the pinned GX (closes #205), and the dashboard KPI enrichment — Avg. Duration + real period-over-period deltas (closes #352); "
       "epic #177 + #128 closed with the close-out).")
assert old in s; s = s.replace(old, new, 1)

old = "**Week-7 exit gate:** Production-ready v1 deployed to Azure, CI/CD live, team onboarded. — **met**"
new = ("**Week-8 exit gate:** ≥80% coverage gate enforced in CI across backend, API, frontend. — **met** (2026-07-03: backend/API gate `--cov-fail-under=80` on every pytest run incl. CI (#557); frontend all-src `lines: 80` via `pnpm test:coverage` in CI (#558); baselines 98.3% / 87.8% at flip).\n"
       "**Week-7 exit gate:** Production-ready v1 deployed to Azure, CI/CD live, team onboarded. — **met**")
assert old in s; s = s.replace(old, new, 1)

old = "**Next milestone:** **Week 8 — Unit testing** (exit gate: ≥80% coverage gate enforced in CI across backend, API, frontend). Week 7 closed"
new = ("**Next milestone:** **v1 COMPLETE — post-v1 cycle.** Week 8 closed **COMPLETE 29/29** on 2026-07-03 (milestone + epic #177 + #128 closed; #556–#560 all squash-merged after /code-review agent passes + green CI). "
       "All post-v1 / deferred work is consolidated in [context/post-v1-roadmap.md](context/post-v1-roadmap.md) (50 backlog issues, themed) — that file is the input for the next cycle's planning. "
       "_(Prior-week detail:_ Week 7 closed")
assert old in s; s = s.replace(old, new, 1)
# close that parenthetical at the end of the Next-milestone paragraph
old = "(incl. ADR 0026 — DataQ-issued API keys / service tokens, #461).\n**Active blockers:**"
assert old in s
s = s.replace(old, "(incl. ADR 0026 — DataQ-issued API keys / service tokens, #461).)_\n**Active blockers:**", 1)

old = "Week-8-milestoned [#352](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/352) (dashboard KPI enrichment) + [#205](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/205) (catalog↔GX contract test); "
new = ""
assert old in s; s = s.replace(old, new, 1)

open(p, 'w').write(s)
print('CLAUDE.md ok')
EOF

1	# DataQ — Week 8 working tracker (Unit testing & coverage ≥80%) + v1 go-live gate
2	
3	> Working tracker, same convention as the retired `WEEK7_TODO.md` (gitignored via `*_TODO.md`, never committed).
4	> Created 2026-07-03. Epic: [#177](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/177) · milestone `Week 8 — Unit testing & coverage (≥80%)`.
5	> Task-level source of truth stays `docs/progress.md` (Week 8 section: **8 ✅ / 20 🟡 / 1 ⬜** after the #552 re-base).
6	> Legend: `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · 🧑 = user-only step · ⚠️ = decision needed
7	
8	**Context:** coverage is already ~91% backend / ~80% frontend lines — Week 8 is NOT "write tests
9	all week." It is (1) a per-module **audit** against the ≥80% bar, (2) closing genuine gaps, (3)
10	**flipping the CI gate**, and (4) the go-live/ops items below so "done" doesn't start decaying
11	the moment attention moves off the project.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## Track A (LEAD) — Coverage audit → CI gate (the exit-gate deliverable)
16	
17	- [ ] **A1 — Measure per-module, don't trust the tracker.** Run `pytest --cov --cov-report=term-missing`
18	      (backend) + `pnpm test -- --coverage` (frontend) and produce a per-file table.
19	      ⚠️ Specifically re-verify the **8 rows #552 flipped to ✅ on file-existence evidence, not
20	      measured coverage** (MCP, secrets, SharePanel, execution-page components, AuthGate,
21	      interceptor, fixtures ×2) — confirm each actually clears 80% or demote back to 🟡.
22	- [ ] **A2 — Close genuine gaps** surfaced by A1 (expect them in: auth deps edge paths, flat-file/UC
23	      live-seam adjacents, error handlers, frontend pages with render-only tests).
24	      One functionality per commit; adversarial battery for anything data-ingesting (rule 4a).
25	- [ ] **A3 — Flip the gate**: backend `--cov-fail-under=80` (pyproject/CI), frontend vitest
26	      `coverage.thresholds` ≥80 lines, wire both into the required CI checks. This is the single
27	      remaining ⬜ roadmap row and the week's exit gate.
28	- [ ] **A4 — Mutation spike** (rule 4a cadence): one `mutmut` run on a core service + one Stryker
29	      run on a core component. History says line coverage lies (~94% still missed the profiler
30	      500s, #145/#147) — the gate alone is not the quality bar.
31	- [ ] **A5 — Use `qa-verifier`** for the pre-push battery + a data-level scenario pass against the
32	      seeded local stack (its first real workout since #548).
33	
34	## Track B — Week-8-milestone issues (5 open)
35	
36	- [ ] **#205** — contract test: expectation-catalog field/type names resolve against pinned GX
37	- [ ] **#352** — dashboard KPI enrichment (avg duration + period-over-period deltas)
38	- [ ] **#385** — CORS middleware activation test (configured vs unset origins)
39	- [ ] **#553** — remove the 5 stale `pip-audit --ignore-vuln` flags (post-#129 they mask nothing)
40	- [ ] **#128** — closes when A3 lands (both E2E halves already shipped; gate is the tail)
41	- [ ] **#177 epic** — tick as rows land; close with the milestone
42	
43	## Track C — Decay clock / ops register (the "what you're missing" items — do DURING W8, not after)
44	
45	> v1 runs on borrowed, expiring foundations. Nothing below is scheduled by any system today.
46	
47	- [ ] **C1 — ⚠️ September credential cluster (the silent multi-system outage in ~12 weeks):**
48	      Snowflake PAT expires **~2026-09-26** (minted 2026-06-28, 90d) · all three Databricks PATs
49	      expire **~2026-09-30** (minted 2026-07-02, 90d: `conn-unity-catalog-retail`, `-qa`,
50	      `databricks-token-harness`). When they lapse, Flows A+B, UC suites, and harness Airflow fail
51	      *together*. → Set a **2026-09-20 "rotate everything" reminder** (calendar / scheduled agent /
52	      cron — an actual timer, not prose). Rotation runbooks: Snowflake PAT via `DATAQ_READER_PAT`
53	      on ROYARIJIT04; Databricks via CLI profile `dataq-free` → `POST /api/2.0/token/create`;
54	      KV `dataq-app-kv-aw6laj` is the source of truth; Airflow ACA needs a restart after the
55	      harness secret update (start-time snapshot).
56	- [ ] **C2 — Give the written cadences timers:** weekly `/security-scan` (CONTRIBUTING rule 36),
57	      quarterly MCP supply-chain audit (rule 39, next ~2026-10-01), Dependabot triage. Decide the
58	      mechanism (scheduled Claude routine / calendar / CI cron) and set it up once.
59	- [ ] **C3 — ⚠️ ADR 0026 decision (API keys / service tokens, #461):** currently every MCP client
60	      config dies hourly with the pasted bearer (the `.vscode/mcp.json` token is already expired).
61	      Decide: pull ADR 0026 into W8 (kills the token-paste problem + shrinks C1) or explicitly
62	      re-affirm post-v1. Record the decision either way.
63	- [ ] **C4 — ⚠️ Databricks Free Edition is NON-COMMERCIAL-ONLY** — and ADR 0013's ambition is

65	      constraint + trigger condition ("before any commercial demo → paid workspace") in the ADR
66	      0013 or 0021 notes so it's discovered before someone asks for a demo, not during.
67	- [ ] **C5 — Bus factor = 1:** no second human has ever operated the system; the runbook is
68	      validated only by its author. Either 🧑 have someone cold-run `deploy/README.md` +
69	      `docs/runbook-faq.md` once, or record "accepted: solo-dev" explicitly.
70	- [ ] **C6 — Long-dated register entries** (verify, don't re-derive): ADLS account SAS expires
71	      **2027-06-28** (⚠️ noted from rotation notes — the SAS string's expiry was never
72	      independently re-read; confirm once during C1 setup) · ADF/Airflow webhook secrets — rotate
73	      on suspicion only (ADR 0006 hard cutover) · KV purge-protection stays **off** (decision
74	      2026-07-02, `deploy/README.md`) — revisit trigger: any regulated/production-critical use.
75	- [ ] **C7 — Watch Free-Edition fair-use quota for the first week:** nightly Flow B now spends
76	      model-serving quota (`ai_analyze_sentiment`, incremental so re-runs are free) — if compute
77	      pauses for a day, trigger-bound suites go stale with no code bug. Know what that failure
78	      looks like before it happens.
79	
80	## Track D — Loose ends (small, do when adjacent)
81	
82	- [ ] **D1 — MCP in-client NL tool-selection spot-check** — the softest tick in W7's 41/41: nobody
83	      has watched an actual LLM client pick the right tool from the 4 canonical queries (I chose
84	      the tools in the E2E). 10 minutes in VS Code Copilot agent mode with a fresh token; file
85	      description fixes as issues if selection misses.
86	- [ ] **D2 — `profile_column` usability** — on SQL suites it 422s without explicit `table`/`schema`
87	      instead of defaulting to the suite's run target (found in the MCP E2E). File + fix or defer
88	      to #529/Theme 13.
89	- [ ] **D3 — Fresh token into `.vscode/mcp.json`** whenever resuming MCP work (current one expired
90	      2026-07-03; 401s are expiry, not a regression).
91	
92	## Go-live checkmarks — ALL must pass before declaring v1 DONE at Week-8 exit
93	
94	- [ ] ☑ **CI coverage gate enforced** — `--cov-fail-under=80` backend + vitest thresholds frontend,
95	      in the required-checks set, green on `main` (the roadmap exit gate, verbatim)
96	- [ ] ☑ **Per-module audit clean** — every Week-8 roadmap row genuinely ≥80% or explicitly
97	      re-triaged with an issue; the 8 #552 ✅ rows re-verified with measured numbers
98	- [ ] ☑ **Mutation spike run** with findings filed (not necessarily fixed) — line coverage alone
99	      is not accepted as the quality bar

102	- [ ] ☑ **Live smoke re-green** after the last deploy of the week (the #531 lane: Flows A/B/C +
103	      bearer-mode `e2e_smoke.py`) — the gate flip must not be the only thing verified
104	- [ ] ☑ **`/security-scan` clean** in the same week as go-live (last run 2026-07-03: 1 finding → #553)
105	- [ ] ☑ **Ops/renewals register exists with real timers** — C1 reminder set, C2 cadences scheduled,
106	      C6 entries confirmed
107	- [ ] ☑ **Two ⚠️ decisions recorded**: ADR 0026 now-vs-post-v1 (C3) + Free-Edition commercial
108	      constraint (C4)
109	- [ ] ☑ **Docs current**: `docs/progress.md` Week-8 section + aggregate final; CLAUDE.md §13 flipped
110	      to "v1 COMPLETE"; README/docs-site claims re-checked against the gate
111	- [ ] ☑ **v1 tagged**: `v1.0.0` git tag + GitHub release notes (the ledger writes itself from

- [ ] **C1 — ⚠️ September credential cluster (the silent multi-system outage in ~12 weeks):**
      Snowflake PAT expires **~2026-09-26** (minted 2026-06-28, 90d) · all three Databricks PATs

## Audit: `docs/w8-close-out` vs `main`

## Cross-check result — the docs PR was corrected before landing

My draft close-out had declared "v1 COMPLETE" — the tracker's go-live gate says otherwise. Corrected: progress.md/CLAUDE.md now say **"roadmap 29/29 + exit gate MET; go-live checklist in flight"**, epic #177 and the milestone stay open, and the remaining tail is named in the repo-visible snapshot (not just the gitignored tracker).

**Done today (Tracks A+B core):**
- **A1–A3** ✅ — per-module audit (the 8 rows #552 flipped on file-existence were implicitly verified: none were below 80%; the four genuine gaps were elsewhere and got fixed), gaps closed, both gates flipped and green on main (98.33%/1273 backend · 87.8%/334 frontend)
- **#385, #205, #352** ✅ closed by #556/#559/#560; **#128** closes when #561 merges

**Caught by the cross-check — still open:**
- **#553** — remove the 5 stale `pip-audit --ignore-vuln` flags (W8-milestoned; filed by the 07-03 security scan *after* the register was last verified — I'd worked off the stale list)
- **A4** — mutation spike (mutmut + Stryker) — the tracker explicitly says line coverage isn't the quality bar
- **A5** — a real qa-verifier workout (I ran the gate battery manually)
- **Track C** — the decay-clock items: the **September credential cluster timer** (Snowflake PAT ~09-26, 3× Databricks PATs ~09-30 — a coordinated multi-system outage in ~12 weeks if nothing is set), cadence timers, the **ADR-0026 decision** (hourly-expiring MCP bearer tokens), the **Databricks Free-Edition non-commercial constraint** recording, bus-factor, SAS confirm, quota watch
- **Track D** — MCP NL tool-selection spot-check (D1), `profile_column` 422 usability (D2)
- **Go-live tail** — prod deploy of the W8 batch + live-smoke re-green, `v1.0.0` tag + release, retro

I also ticked the completed items inside `WEEK8_TODO.md` itself (stays local/gitignored). The docs-consistency-guard audited #561 — one real finding (malformed italic wraps) fixed and pushed; its #128 concern is the intended post-merge state.

I'll merge #561 when CI reports green. After that, the natural next moves in tracker order: **#553** (small CI PR), then the **mutation spike**, then the **prod deploy + smoke re-green** — with Track C's timers and the two decisions needing your input on mechanism (scheduled agent vs calendar) and direction (ADR 0026 now vs post-v1).

content.content:
## Finding (weekly /security-scan, 2026-07-03)

The `backend-audit` CI job pins 5 ignored advisories (`PYSEC-2026-36`, `PYSEC-2026-35`, `CVE-2026-26007`, `CVE-2026-27448`, `CVE-2026-27459`) — cryptography/pyOpenSSL advisories that were blocked by `snowflake-connector-python<4`, with #129 named as the removal deadline.

#129 closed 2026-06-08 (ADR 0017 — Snowflake 3→4 + the CVE refresh). A bare `pip-audit -r backend/requirements-dev.txt` (no ignores) now reports **No known vulnerabilities found** — the ignore list masks nothing today, but a stale allowlist is drift risk: if any of those IDs ever re-fires against a future dependency state it would be silently suppressed.

## Fix

Delete the five `--ignore-vuln` flags (and the now-inaccurate comment block) from the `backend-audit` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`. Verify the job stays green.

_Found by the `/security-scan` weekly checklist (CONTRIBUTING rule 36); non-sensitive hardening item per rule 38 routing._

# Synchronous CVE gate over the entire runtime + test dependency tree
        # (single source: requirements-dev.txt) + transitive deps, against the
        # PyPI/OSV advisory DB. Complements the Dependabot alerts (full graph,
        # async) with a fail-fast merge check.
        #
        # The 5 ignored advisories are cryptography + pyOpenSSL CVEs blocked by
        # snowflake-connector-python<4 / snowflake-sqlalchemy<4 — a deliberate
        # major datasource bump tracked in #129 (the deadline for removing these).
        #
        # Bounded retry: pip-audit resolves the requirements file via PyPI's
        # per-package JSON endpoint, so a single transient PyPI/OSV 5xx (e.g.
        # "503 Backend is unhealthy") aborts the whole audit and flakes the gate.
        # Retrying with backoff absorbs that *without* masking real findings — a
        # genuine CVE fails every attempt and still blocks (90s worst-case backoff).
        run: |
          audit() {
            pip-audit -r backend/requirements-dev.txt \
              --ignore-vuln PYSEC-2026-36 \
              --ignore-vuln PYSEC-2026-35 \
              --ignore-vuln CVE-2026-26007 \
              --ignore-vuln CVE-2026-27448 \
              --ignore-vuln CVE-2026-27459
          }

content.content:
- name: pip-audit (full runtime + test surface)
        # Synchronous CVE gate over the entire runtime + test dependency tree
        # (single source: requirements-dev.txt) + transitive deps, against the
        # PyPI/OSV advisory DB. Complements the Dependabot alerts (full graph,
        # async) with a fail-fast merge check.
        #
        # The 5 ignored advisories are cryptography + pyOpenSSL CVEs blocked by
        # snowflake-connector-python<4 / snowflake-sqlalchemy<4 — a deliberate
        # major datasource bump tracked in #129 (the deadline for removing these).
        #
        # Bounded retry: pip-audit resolves the requirements file via PyPI's
        # per-package JSON endpoint, so a single transient PyPI/OSV 5xx (e.g.
        # "503 Backend is unhealthy") aborts the whole audit and flakes the gate.
        # Retrying with backoff absorbs that *without* masking real findings — a
        # genuine CVE fails every attempt and still blocks (90s worst-case backoff).
        run: |
          audit() {
            pip-audit -r backend/requirements-dev.txt \
              --ignore-vuln PYSEC-2026-36 \
              --ignore-vuln PYSEC-2026-35 \
              --ignore-vuln CVE-2026-26007 \
              --ignore-vuln CVE-2026-27448 \
              --ignore-vuln CVE-2026-27459
          }
          for attempt in 1 2 3 4; do
            if audit; then
              exit 0
            fi
            if [ "$attempt" -lt 4 ]; then
              echo "::warning::pip-audit attempt $attempt failed (likely a transient PyPI/OSV 5xx) — retrying in $((attempt * 15))s"
              sleep $((attempt * 15))
            fi
          done
          echo "::error::pip-audit failed after 4 attempts — a real CVE or a persistent PyPI/OSV outage (see log above)"
          exit 1

content.content:
# Frontend mutation testing (Stryker)

The frontend analogue of the backend mutmut spike ([CONTRIBUTING rule 4a](../../CONTRIBUTING.md)).
**Manual / periodic, never CI** — mutation testing is too slow to gate merges, and
its big tool tree shouldn't sit on the `pnpm audit` surface. So, like the backend's
standalone `requirements-mutation.txt`, **Stryker is not a dependency in
`frontend/package.json`** — [`run.sh`](./run.sh) installs it ad-hoc (pinned) and
restores the manifest on exit.

## Run a spike

```bash
frontend/mutation/run.sh                                  # mutate the configured target
frontend/mutation/run.sh --mutate 'src/components/checks/checkForm.ts'
```

Config lives in [`../stryker.conf.json`](../stryker.conf.json); the version pin is in
`run.sh`. The default `mutate` target is `suiteTarget.ts` — point it at whatever pure
module you're hardening (the conversion/resolver utils are the best targets:
`suiteTarget.ts`, `checkForm.ts`, `expectationCatalog.ts`, `resultsFormat.ts`).

## Reading the result

Stryker prints a `[Survived]` block per mutant the tests failed to kill, plus a
mutation-score table. Triage each survivor:

- **Real gap** — a behavioural mutation (flipped condition, dropped branch, changed
  return) that no test caught. Add/strengthen a test. This is the point of the spike.
- **Equivalent mutant** — a change with no observable effect (e.g. mutating a value
  that's re-narrowed downstream, or an unreachable branch). Not a gap; leave it.
/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/requirements-mutation.txt:4:# mutmut and its transitive deps (libcst, parso, textual, …) stay OFF the CI
/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/requirements-mutation.txt:10:# 3.x is a ground-up rewrite (config in pyproject `[tool.mutmut]`, copies the tree
/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/requirements-mutation.txt:12:# `mutmut results`/`show` on Python 3.13 (#253), so reporting works natively again.
/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/requirements-mutation.txt:16:mutmut==3.6.0

3:# Manual-only for now (workflow_dispatch): apply-ready scaffolding that never
4-# fires on its own until the Azure resources exist + secrets/vars are set. Once
5-# the infra is provisioned (see deploy/README.md), flip on the push trigger:
6-#   push:
7-#     branches: [main]
8-on:
9:  workflow_dispatch:
10-    inputs:
11-      image_tag:
12-        description: "Image tag to build + deploy (blank → the commit SHA, an immutable tag)"
13-        default: ""
14-
15-concurrency:
16-  group: deploy-production
17-  cancel-in-progress: false
18-
19-# OIDC federated login to Azure (no stored client secret). The federated cred +
20-# the listed vars/secrets must be configured first — see deploy/README.md.
21-# packages:write lets the GITHUB_TOKEN push the backend image to GHCR (ADR 0023).
22-permissions:
23-  id-token: write
24-  contents: read
25-  packages: write
26-
27-env:
28-  # GHCR registry (ADR 0023) — public packages, lowercase owner. ACA pulls them
29-  # anonymously, so no registry credential is stored on the apps/job.

gh issue create --title "test: mutation-spike findings (W8 A4) — resultsFormat colour maps unasserted (82.35% score); dashboard_service 100%" --label testing --milestone "Backlog (post-v1 / testing)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Week-8 rule-4a mutation spike (go-live checklist item)

Two spikes run 2026-07-03 on this week's new/core code:

### mutmut — `backend/app/services/dashboard_service.py`
**436/436 mutants killed, 0 survived** (`mutmut results` empty), driven by `tests/services/test_dashboard_service.py` (31 tests incl. the parametrized health-score/pass-rate/state batteries + the #560 delta/duration tests). No action needed; recorded as the strongest module measured so far.

### Stryker — `frontend/src/components/results/resultsFormat.ts`
**Mutation score 82.35%** (84 killed · **14 survived** · **4 no-coverage** · 0 timeout/error).

Surviving class (from the clear-text report): **StringLiteral mutations in the status→antd-colour maps** — e.g. `running: 'processing'`, `cancelled: 'warning'` (RUN_STATUS_COLORS) and `pass: 'success'`, `fail: 'error'`, `skip: 'default'` (RESULT_STATUS_COLORS) each mutate to `""` with all tests green. The vitest suite exercises the formatters (`formatDuration(Ms)`, `formatScalar`, `formatTimestamp`, window logic) but treats the colour maps as data nobody asserts — a wrong badge colour for any status would ship silently. 4 mutants sit on uncovered lines.

## Suggested fix
- Table-driven assertion over every entry of `RUN_STATUS_COLORS` / `RESULT_STATUS_COLORS` / `RUN_BAR_STATUS` / `pipelineStatusColor` (exact expected antd colour per status — these are UI contract, not incidental data), which also picks up the 4 no-coverage lines.
- Re-run `frontend/mutation/run.sh --mutate 'src/components/results/resultsFormat.ts'` and confirm the score clears ~95%+.

Per the W8 tracker: findings **filed**, not required fixed, for the go-live gate. Repro: `pyproject.toml [tool.mutmut]` currently points at `dashboard_service.py`; Stryker via `frontend/mutation/run.sh --mutate 'src/components/results/resultsFormat.ts'`.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

Two spikes run 2026-07-03 on this week's new/core code:

### mutmut — `backend/app/services/dashboard_service.py`
**436/436 mutants killed, 0 survived** (`mutmut results` empty), driven by `tests/services/test_dashboard_service.py` (31 tests incl. the parametrized health-score/pass-rate/state batteries + the #560 delta/duration tests). No action needed; recorded as the strongest module measured so far.

### Stryker — `frontend/src/components/results/resultsFormat.ts`
**Mutation score 82.35%** (84 killed · **14 survived** · **4 no-coverage** · 0 timeout/error).

Surviving class (from the clear-text report): **StringLiteral mutations in the status→antd-colour maps** — e.g. `running: 'processing'`, `cancelled: 'warning'` (RUN_STATUS_COLORS) and `pass: 'success'`, `fail: 'error'`, `skip: 'default'` (RESULT_STATUS_COLORS) each mutate to `""` with all tests green. The vitest suite exercises the formatters (`formatDuration(Ms)`, `formatScalar`, `formatTimestamp`, window logic) but treats the colour maps as data nobody asserts — a wrong badge colour for any status would ship silently. 4 mutants sit on uncovered lines.

## Suggested fix
- Table-driven assertion over every entry of `RUN_STATUS_COLORS` / `RESULT_STATUS_COLORS` / `RUN_BAR_STATUS` / `pipelineStatusColor` (exact expected antd colour per status — these are UI contract, not incidental data), which also picks up the 4 no-coverage lines.
- Re-run `frontend/mutation/run.sh --mutate 'src/components/results/resultsFormat.ts'` and confirm the score clears ~95%+.

Per the W8 tracker: findings **filed**, not required fixed, for the go-live gate. Repro: `pyproject.toml [tool.mutmut]` currently points at `dashboard_service.py`; Stryker via `frontend/mutation/run.sh --mutate 'src/components/results/resultsFormat.ts'`.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1
